2604.07084.title: Flow Motion Policy: Manipulator Motion Planning with Flow Matching Models
2604.07084.paper_summary: Open-loop end-to-end neural motion planners have recently been proposed to improve motion planning for robotic manipulators. These methods enable planning directly from sensor observations without relying on a privileged collision checker during planning. However, many existing methods generate only a single path for a given workspace across different runs, and do not leverage their open-loop structure for inference-time optimization. To address this limitation, we introduce Flow Motion Policy, an open-loop, end-to-end neural motion planner for robotic manipulators that leverages the stochastic generative formulation of flow matching methods to capture the inherent multi-modality of planning datasets. By modeling a distribution over feasible paths, Flow Motion Policy enables efficient inference-time best-of-$N$ sampling. The method generates multiple end-to-end candidate paths, evaluates their collision status after planning, and executes the first collision-free solution. We benchmark the Flow Motion Policy against representative sampling-based and neural motion planning methods. Evaluation results demonstrate that Flow Motion Policy improves planning success and efficiency, highlighting the effectiveness of stochastic generative policies for end-to-end motion planning and inference-time optimization. Experimental evaluation videos are available via this \href{https://zh.engr.tamu.edu/wp-content/uploads/sites/310/2026/03/FMP-Website.mp4}{link}.
2604.07084.paper_summary_zh: 開環端到端神經運動規劃器最近被提出以改善機器人操作手的運動規劃。<lb/>這些方法允許直接從傳感器觀察中進行規劃，而不依賴於特權的碰撞檢查器。<lb/>然而，許多現有的方法在不同的運行中僅生成一條單一的路徑，並未利用其開環結構進行推斷時的優化。<lb/>為了解決這一限制，我們引入了流動運動策略（Flow Motion Policy），這是一種開環的端到端神經運動規劃器，專為機器人操作手設計，利用流匹配方法的隨機生成形式來捕捉規劃數據集的內在多模態性。<lb/>通過對可行路徑建模分佈，流動運動策略實現了高效的推斷時最佳的$N$抽樣。<lb/>該方法生成多條端到端候選路徑，在規劃後評估其碰撞狀態，並執行第一個無碰撞的解決方案。<lb/>我們將流動運動策略與代表性的基於抽樣和神經運動規劃方法進行基準測試。<lb/>評估結果表明，流動運動策略提高了規劃的成功率和效率，突顯了隨機生成策略在端到端運動規劃和推斷時優化中的有效性。<lb/>實驗評估視頻可通過此 \href{https://zh.engr.tamu.edu/wp-content/uploads/sites/310/2026/03/FMP-Website.mp4}{鏈接} 獲得。
2604.07084.author: Davood Soleymanzadeh et.al.
2604.07084.authors: Davood Soleymanzadeh, Xiao Liang, Minghui Zheng
